summary: Decent sound with some muddy moments, but a strong second set highlighted by Shakedown, Terrapin and an Iko Iko > Black Peter sequence.
review: The recording is generally listenable though a few reviewers note mud on the Shakedown and occasional mic‑clap bleed, suggesting a mixed‑quality source. Musically the show shines in its second set – Jerry’s funky Shakedown solo, a solid Terrapin, and an energetic Iko Iko → Black Peter → Round & Round stretch receive high praise, while some feel the early jams drag.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers highlight strong performances and several standout songs (Shakedown, Terrapin, Iko Iko sequence), but recurring comments about muddy sound and uneven set flow lower the overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Occasional mud on the Shakedown, mic‑clap bleed noted; overall decent clarity but not pristine.

standout_songs: Iko Iko > Black Peter > Round & Round
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is missing for some listeners, but the second set delivers high energy peaks with Shakedown and Terrapin before a dip at Iko, then recovers with the Black Peter/Round & Round stretch.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Funky, out‑of‑this‑world solo on Shakedown praised; some listeners note his guitar is missing in two songs.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith and Donna receive positive remarks for their keyboards throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums segment noted for a late ‘procession’ feel and enjoyed as part of Playin>Drums>Space.
